Hey everybody, Frank Kern here and welcome to this training. This is going to be awesome. Okay, so I'm going to keep the introduction parts really, really brief, but there is some stuff that's super important. Thing number one is the student mix in this training is about 6040 with 60% being offer owners, meaning people are going through the certification to literally learn how to do this stuff for their own business. And then the other 40 is agencies and consultants and service providers. So because of that, I'm going to use phrases like, Hey, here's what you should do with a client. And I'll also use phrases like, Hey, here's what you should do in your business. At the end of the day, it's all the same thing, whether you're your own client or you're dealing with someone else's business as them being your client, our mission as a result of you going through this is to be able to generate customers and revenue on demand consistently at scale, regardless of who the business owner is. But I did want to give you a heads up there, because I don't want to confuse you if you hear me start talking about, Hey, here's what to do with a client. And you're in this to work with your own business. You're like, What is he talking about? It's all the same thing. All right, so that's thing number one, thing number two is how the overall Rainmaker process works. So there are three steps to this. And I follow these steps in my business when I'm looking to grow revenues. And this is also the same thing that we do for agency clients, any done for you capacity. And this is also the same thing we do when we're dealing with consulting clients who pay high fee consulting stuff. And our three steps that we go through are essentially this. Number one is find the low hanging fruit. All right, so generally speaking, doesn't matter what type of business it is, the business owner that could be me often is me, could be you, could be your client, whoever, the business owner is so busy running the business and like doing stuff and the business that for lack of a better phrase, they can't see the forest through the trees. And they don't realize that there's a lot of low hanging fruit opportunities that they could just take advantage of right now to get immediate revenue for. So for the first part of our work together, that's what we're going to be focused on, especially this week, and this particular segment, and a lot of next week's segment, it's like, okay, let's get immediate revenue and let's increase the revenue from the leads and traffic that you're getting right now. So that's phase number one. And we always want to start there. The second phase is to take whatever we're doing this working and make sure that it's evergreen and it's systemized. And I always want to say systematized, but I used Google and the real word is systemized, who knew, right? But we always want to systemize whatever is working. And the reason this is important, you may have done this in your business, you may have clients who've done this, I know I've done it only like all the time, which is you'll do something that works really, really well. And then something will happen and you'll stop doing it. So what we want to do is we want to take what's working really, really well, and we want to systemize it and we want to make it to where it'll always work and can always be deployed. So whatever is getting the most revenue from your traffic and your leads, that's what's going to be put in front of your traffic and your leads first, whether that's a specific funnel or a specific process in your funnel, or a specific piece of your follow ups or whatever. Okay, so that's our second phase. Now, when we do all of that, step one, which is to find the low-hanging fruit, step two, which is to systemize the getting of the low-hanging fruit, then it's time to move to the third phase, which is everybody's favorite, which is ads and scale. All right. And so when we talk about ads in this class, we're going to be doing Facebook ads, you're going to be doing YouTube ads, you're going to be doing a multi-platform retargeting, including but not limited to display and all kinds of cool stuff. I really, really urge you, I know ads are really everybody's favorite, but I really, really urge you to pay strong attention to the first sections, because if you have that stuff dialed in, if you've got everything programmed to like take advantage of the lowest-hanging fruit to maximize every lead that's coming into your sales process, it just makes the ads work better. Right? So that's kind of part of the big secret. Like, if you think about it, ads are just words and pictures put on a screen in front of the right person at the right time is what happens after they click, which is when the money is made. So those are our three main areas of focus during the certification. You're going to get graded all three of these things, and you're actually going to have the ability to generate revenues and increase revenues, maximize revenues, do so consistently and predictably across your business, clients, businesses, any business that you choose, which is a really, really valuable skill to have. Now, there are a few unsexy secrets that make all this stuff work. There are unsexy secrets that make it where our agency is able to go out and say, hey, don't pay us, you know, or we will earn you more than you pay us or else we'll refund the difference plus 20%. There are very unsexy secrets that allow us to get those types of results for people. And the reason I'm framing these as unsexy secrets is because none of the stuff that I'm about to show you real briefly is a shiny, cool object. In fact, this is stuff that a lot of people avoid, but this is where the rubber meets the road. So unsexy secret number one is the idea of doing everything holistically. All right, so you've undoubtedly seen a billion webinars and all kinds of courses, books, ads, whatever on here's how to make Facebook work better. Or, you know, Facebook's dead, now it's YouTube. Or here's how to do emails that work or here's how to do affiliate marketing or whatever. The fact of the matter is focusing on any one of those things is like building a stool with one leg. At the end of the day, to have a business that is consistently profitable and like really profitable and consistently, you know, maximize in terms of generating as much revenue as possible for you, you really need to be using all of the tools at your disposal. So what that means is the ads need to be good. Yeah, of course. All right, the targeting needs to be good. That's gotten a million times easier, thank God. Also, the offer itself needs to be good. You know, your landing page needs to be good. The stuff that you're using to get people to opt into your list needs to be attracting the right people. Your sales process needs to be good. If you don't have order bombs, if you don't have one click up sales and your sales process merits it, you need to have all of those things and your email marketing needs to be good. So one of the unsexy secrets about this is we actually do all of that stuff during this certification. And the logic here is you really have two choices. You can go down this one rabbit hole and try to be unbelievably good at one of those things, which is what everybody does. Like, okay, I'm going to be really, really good at copy or I'm going to be unbelievably good at the ad part or I'm going to be really, really good at conversion rate optimization or whatever. And getting exceptionally good, hard to say exceptionally there, getting exceptionally good at any one of those things is extremely hard and takes forever. But if you just do a pretty good job at all of those things, so if you just get like incremental improvement across all of those things, like your ads, you know, you get to like lower cost per click, you get cheaper customer acquisitions, if you just increase your average upfront order value a little bit, you know, if you just get a couple of percentage points more customers from follow up, if you just get a couple percentage points more repeat business and all of those types of things, that stuff adds up to just unbelievable exponential growth in your company and doing a pretty good job at all of that stuff. There's like a thousand times easier than becoming an unbelievable world class expert in one little area and one little area is not where you want to be an expert at. Anyway, you want to use everything holistically. So that is unsexy secret number one. Unsexy secret number two to making all of this stuff work is process. So every business out there, we're going to take a really deep dive into this, every business out there has what's called a sales process. And the sales process is a fancy way of saying a way of taking a stranger and turning them into a customer. And every single sales process has specific steps that can be maximized in order to make stuff work better. Right? So a huge unsexy secret about all of this is I'm going to hand you all of these processes and all of the checklists and all of the process maps on the silver platter. So you'll just be able to be like, Oh, okay, well, I want to increase profits in this business. This is a, let's say a webinar funnel, and I'm going through the checklist and seems like this one little part of the process is underperforming. What does it say to do to make that work better? I'm going to turn that little dial and I'm going to make that one thing to work better. Now, that's not a big sexy, you know, crazy change. But when you know exactly how a sales process is supposed to work, you know, defined by industry, and again, I'll give you all of these. And then you know how to improve it, you know exactly what to do to improve it. These little improvements, they all add up and it all really, really makes a huge difference, which is great. The third profoundly unsexy secret, maybe even the least sexy secret of it all, is math. So I've been doing this a long time. I've been consulting with businesses since 1999. I've had my own companies since 99, a lot of customers, a lot of different business types. So many of us make decisions here, right, about like where our passion is or what we think should be working or, you know, we rarely make decisions based on math. No, I don't mean like advanced mathematics. I mean the type of math you could do with your phone, you know, the calculator on your phone. What we're going to do in terms of getting you money quickly, making you money more consistently and more and more over time and making everything work, whether it is for you or for your clients, is we're going to make all of our decisions based on math. It takes the emotion out of it and it really makes it hard for us to make a mistake because the math is almost always right if you follow it. Now our final unsexy secret, and this might actually be the secret weapon, and that is the power of diagnostics. I know that already sounds like a sleeper, right? So here's the thing, okay? When stuff is working, it's really easy to be like, yeah, all right, this is great. I'm a genius. When stuff is not working, it's equally as easy to be like, oh man, nothing works. I got to change everything. What you're going to find, whether you're working in your own business or whether you're working in a client's business, is that a huge opportunity to get more money immediately is understanding how to diagnose where the missing revenue is, whether it's a broken sales process, like maybe their business isn't working or maybe their business is working okay, but you can do some diagnostics. Just the word itself is boring. I know you can do some diagnostics and you can find out where the hidden money is. So one of the huge skills that you're going to learn, and again, this translates directly to your own business and or for clients business, is how to take a look at any business and how to look at all the moving pieces and be like, oh, okay, that's what needs fixing. And even if it's not broken, you'll be able to be like, oh, you know, if we change that to this and we put a little thing over here, that's going to make this business really, really take off. So those are your unsexy secrets. Boy, am I doing a great job of selling your participation in this, right? But I just want to shoot you straight. What we're going to be doing is we're going to be doing stuff that gets money. A lot of the stuff that gets money is plain old common sense. It's good old fashioned common sense, math and the ability to diagnose situations and make decisions not based on emotion, but based on what has historically worked and what the numbers are telling us. And the good news is, there's a roadmap for every single piece of this puzzle. There's a roadmap for what your landing pages should look like. There's a roadmap for how your sales processes should be. There's a roadmap for what to say. There's a roadmap for your email sequences. There's a roadmap for everything. And this certification, I'm handing you the roadmaps, I'm going to make sure that you know them and you know exactly how to implement them.
